city_personality.slang_reaction: Yeah, nah, that's a bit average, mate.

opening_sequence.title: Welcome to Brissy, Lord Mayor
opening_sequence.intro: The River City is booming, the Olympics are coming, and the budget is healthy. But housing prices are locking out locals, and the threat of floods always looms.

advisors.name: Sarah Jenkins
advisors.hidden_agenda: Privatizing council assets to build a massive surplus before the state election.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Increasing council rates to fund public services.

briefing: We've got a healthy surplus this quarter.
Olympics funding from the feds came through.
The property market is keeping revenues high.
dialogue.betrayal: You blew the surplus. I'm taking a job in the private sector where math still matters.
roast: Your budget looks like a pub tab on a Friday night.
You couldn't manage a sausage sizzle.
scandal_reaction: This will impact our credit rating.

advisors.name: Senior Sergeant Craig O'Donovan
advisors.hidden_agenda: Implementing highly restrictive lockout laws in Fortitude Valley.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Legalizing extended trading hours for nightclubs and bars.

briefing: Quiet weekend in the Valley, thankfully.
Youth crime in the outer suburbs is making headlines.
SES is on standby for the storm season.
disagreement: The Courier-Mail will have a field day with this.
dialogue.betrayal: You care more about the party scene than public safety. I'm done.
roast: You're softer on crime than a wet sponge.
This city is turning into a circus.

advisors.name: Dr. Liam Nguyen
advisors.hidden_agenda: Banning all new development on flood plains, regardless of economic cost.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Approving a massive luxury subdivision in a known flood zone.

briefing: Another high-rise proposal for West End.
We need more green space in the CBD.
The river health report is concerning.
dialogue.betrayal: You're selling out our environment to developers. I'm running against you next election.
roast: Your urban plan is just a giant car park.
You'd concrete the river if you could.
scandal_reaction: I strongly advise against this course.

advisors.name: Emma Wilson
advisors.hidden_agenda: Pushing through a controversial new toll tunnel project tied to her former employer.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Scrapping the proposed tunnel in favor of heavily subsidized public transport.

briefing: The Cross River Rail is slightly behind schedule.
M1 traffic is banked up to the Gold Coast.
disagreement: You're going to create a bottleneck.
dialogue.betrayal: You scrapped the infrastructure we need. Enjoy the gridlock.
roast: Your transport policy is stuck in first gear.
You couldn't organize a bus timetable.
scandal_reaction: Let's just keep working and ignore the noise.

advisors.name: Reverend Peter Davies
advisors.hidden_agenda: Protecting historical, low-density 'character homes' from being bulldozed for apartments.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Abolishing zoning protections to allow high-density apartments in traditional suburbs.

briefing: The community groups are worried about the new developments.
We need to preserve our Queenslander heritage.
The local sports clubs need funding.
dialogue.betrayal: You've destroyed the character of this city. We will vote you out.
roast: You have no respect for tradition.
You're turning Brisbane into a soulless copy of everywhere else.

decisions.title: The Olympic Stadium Dilemma
decisions.body: The Gabba needs a massive, multi-billion dollar rebuild for the upcoming Olympics, but locals are furious about the cost and disruption.

options.label: Proceed with the expensive rebuild as planned.

consequences.public_reaction: Waste of money, but I guess we need it for the Games.

options.label: Scrap the rebuild and use existing, smaller venues.

consequences.public_reaction: Finally some common sense! Save the money.

options.label: Build an entirely new mega-stadium on protected parkland.

consequences.public_reaction: You destroyed our parks for a two-week event?!

decisions.title: The Housing Squeeze
decisions.body: Rent is skyrocketing and locals are being priced out. Developers want to bulldoze low-density 'character' suburbs to build high-rise apartments.

options.label: Maintain current zoning to protect 'Queenslander' homes.

consequences.public_reaction: Glad my street won't be overshadowed, but kids still can't afford rent.

options.label: Abolish zoning restrictions to allow rapid high-density construction.

consequences.public_reaction: Brisbane is turning into Sydney. Gross.

options.label: Implement aggressive rent controls and heavily tax landlords.

consequences.public_reaction: The investors are crying, I love it.

decisions.title: The E-Scooter Menace
decisions.body: Electric scooters are everywhere. They are convenient for commuters but pedestrians are getting injured and they are being thrown into the Brisbane River.

options.label: Increase fines for riding on footpaths.

consequences.public_reaction: Good, I'm tired of almost being run over.

options.label: Ban e-scooters completely from the city.
Remove speed limits on scooters to encourage faster commutes.

consequences.public_reaction: Hospital emergency wards are going to be busy!

decisions.title: Fortitude Valley Lockout Laws
decisions.body: Police want strict 1 AM lockout laws to curb alcohol-fueled violence in the entertainment precinct, but businesses say it will kill the night-time economy.

options.label: Implement the lockout laws.

consequences.public_reaction: Brisbane goes to bed at 9 PM now.

options.label: Keep venues open but heavily increase police presence and ID scanning.

consequences.public_reaction: Fair compromise. Let us party safely.

options.label: Declare a 24-hour party zone with no restrictions.

consequences.public_reaction: The Valley is absolutely feral right now. Amazing.

decisions.title: Cross River Rail Budget Blowout
decisions.body: The massive underground rail project is billions over budget. The state government wants the council to cover the shortfall.

options.label: Pay the shortfall by increasing council rates slightly.

consequences.public_reaction: Rates go up every year, what else is new?

options.label: Refuse to pay, forcing the state to scale back the project.

consequences.public_reaction: Good! Don't let the state government bully us.

options.label: Privatize the entire rail network to cover the costs.

consequences.public_reaction: Fares are going to be fifty bucks a trip now!

crises.name: One-in-100-Year Flood
crises.trigger: A massive rain event has caused the Brisbane River to break its banks, threatening thousands of homes.
crises.description: It's 2011 all over again. Suburbs are going under. The Wivenhoe Dam is at 150% capacity and needs to release water, which will flood the city further. The 'Mud Army' of volunteers is waiting for orders.

options.label: Order immediate mass evacuations and deploy emergency services.
Refuse to release dam water to protect the city, risking a catastrophic dam failure.
Blame the state government for mismanagement while doing nothing.

crises.name: Youth Crime Epidemic Peaks
crises.trigger: A series of high-profile car thefts and home invasions by teenagers has the city in a panic.
crises.description: The Courier-Mail is running front-page campaigns daily. Talkback radio is furious. Citizens are demanding action after a stolen car crashed into a local bakery. The police say their hands are tied by youth justice laws.

options.label: Fund community outreach programs and youth centers.
Implement controversial youth curfews in hotspot suburbs.
Publicly name and shame underage offenders on the council website.

crises.name: Olympics Funding Pulled
crises.trigger: The federal government threatens to pull all funding for the Brisbane Olympics due to your mismanagement.
crises.description: This is a disaster. The city has already started tearing up infrastructure for the games. If the funding doesn't come through, the city will be bankrupt and the global embarrassment will be immense.

options.label: Submit to a federal oversight committee to get the money back.
Threaten to sue the federal government and launch a massive PR campaign.
Cancel the Olympics entirely and keep the unspent funds for local projects.

crises.name: The Property Developer ICAC Probe
crises.trigger: The anti-corruption commission has raided City Hall over your links to major property developers.
crises.description: They are seizing hard drives. The media is swarming the building. Allegations suggest you fast-tracked approvals in exchange for election campaign donations.

options.label: Cooperate fully with the inquiry and throw your staff under the bus.
Refuse to step down, claiming it's a politically motivated witch hunt.
Destroy the evidence in a 'mysterious' server room fire.

scandals.title: Mayor's 'Bogans and Billionaires' Party Costume Outrage
scandals.description: Photos surfaced of you at a private party wearing a highly offensive costume mocking the working class.

scandals.title: Council Sports Grants Funneled to Marginal Seats
scandals.description: Audits show community sports grants were overwhelmingly given to wealthy suburbs in marginal electorates, ignoring poorer areas.

scandals.title: Mayor Caught on Tape Accepting Brown Paper Bag
scandals.description: Hidden camera footage shows you literally accepting a brown paper bag of cash from a major casino developer at a local pub.

positive: Actually a decent idea for once.
Good to see some common sense.
Yeah, I can get behind that.
Brisbane is looking good these days.
neutral: As long as rates don't go up.
Just typical politicians doing politician things.
I just want to get to the Goldy on the weekend.
negative: Can't afford a house, can't afford rent, what are you doing?
Another dumb idea from City Hall.
media: Courier-Mail: OUTRAGE over Council's latest move.
ABC Brisbane: Mixed reactions to the Mayor's announcement.
Channel 9: Commuter chaos as new policy implemented.
Brisbane Times: What this means for your council rates.
Triple M: The Mayor is copping it on talkback today.
Sky News: Is this the worst council decision ever?
politician: The opposition condemns this reckless spending.
We are delivering for the people of Brisbane.
This is an attack on the suburbs.
activist: Housing is a human right, not a commodity.
Climate action now, Brisbane will drown!
Listen to the community, not the developers.
street: Mate, the Bruce Highway is a car park today.
Just paid 800 bucks a week for a dump in Coorparoo.
Anyone going to the Gabba this weekend?
E-scooters are a menace, someone threw one in the river.
Too bloody hot for this nonsense.
Need a beer after dealing with this traffic.
crisis: Get the tinnies out, it's flooding again.
Stay safe everyone, it's getting dangerous.
Why do we keep building on flood plains?
scandal: Crooked as a dog's hind leg.
Straight to ICAC with this one.

scandal_reactions.moderate: That's pretty dodgy, even for a politician.
scandal_reactions.major: Get the corruption watchdog in there right now.
scandal_reactions.career_ending: You're done, mate. Hand in your keys.

romance_exposure.flavour: Australians mostly don't care about their politicians' private lives, making it a subject of mild mockery rather than a career-ending scandal.
